DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-06-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Nick T. Reckas, Esq. (415-464-0913; nreckaslaw@aol.com) of Law Offices of Nick T. Reckas for Plaintiff.
Michael Thomas, Esq. (916-789-1201; mthomas@thomas-lawyers.com) of Thomas & Associates for Defendants.

Not argued. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS MARTIN LUTHER KING MARCUS GARVEY SQUARE, DEDZRA HEWITT, DANIEL LANDRY, AL REDMOND and SAMMIE BROADNAX'S DEMURRER to the First Amended COMPLAINT is overruled.

Defendants' demurrer to the first cause of action against the Directors individually is overruled. Code of Civil Procedure Sec. 425.15(a) states, "[n]o cause of action against a person serving without compensation as a director or officer of a nonprofit corporation described in this section, on account of any negligent act or omission by that person within the scope of that person's duties as a director acting in the capacity of a board member." (Code Civ. Proc. Sec. 425.15(a)(emphasis added).) In this case, Plaintiffs' claims against Defendants allege intentional breaches of fiduciary duty, not "negligent acts or omissions." Plaintiffs' counsel's declaration purporting to raise additional factual allegations is not properly considered by the Court on demurrer.

The Court also rejects Defendant's argument that the statute of limitations has run on any challenge to the results of the 2019 elections under Civil Code Sec. 5145 and Corporations Code Sec. 7527. Civil Code Sec. 5145 provides that a member of an association subject to the Davis-Stirling Common Interest Development Act, Civ. Code Sections 4000-6150, may bring a civil action for declaratory or equitable relief for a violation of Article 4 of that Act "within one year of the deate that the inspector or inspectors of elections notifies the board and member of the election results or the cause of action accrues, whichever is later." As this Court previously stated, "Defendants' brief argument that the statute of limitation has run on the 'gravamen' of the first amended complaint ignores the fact that the results of the 2019 election were never certified as being finalized, and that the defendant's board of directors never held a 2020 election." (Aug. 4, 2021 Order Denying Defendants' Motion to Post Bond Pursuant to Corp. Code Sec. 5710, at 3:25-28 (emphasis added).) Thus, the 2019 Election results were never finalized, and the statute of limitations period has not begun to run.

Prevailing party to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312 via email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)
